The food is very good for it's price, better than most fried dumplings I've had.
Other guests enjoyed pizza, santa fe chopped salad and fish and chips.
We were in search of food and stumbled on this block of Indian restaurants on East Sixth Street.
The fish was not fresh and the rice tasted old and stale.

While this isn't classical restaurant fare, the chef has given new life to an old cuisine with some really innovative and tasty dishes that are genuinely Indian without being heavy or same old restaurant burn-outs.
People are always friendly.
We've never had any problems with the staff but maybe we've been lucky?
Authentic food and they have REAL service, not just the rush you get sometimes when they try to push you out the door.
Our waiter was fine, the place looks nice in that not-trying-too-hard way, but at those prices, a little more should be expected of your food.
The atmosphere isn't the greatest , but I suppose that's how they keep the prices down .
The server was really cool and served us our food and drinks with a smile.
I highly recommend visiting this restaurant and having dinner and drinks!
But, they were too big for the bun.
Also a little more expensive than your average bagel place.
This is a cute place and could be good but they need to get their act together.
This place has the the correct ambience and an excellent staff to make you feel like a guest and a friend at the same time.
It was so bad I actually refused to pay for my food.
Over time, the food quality has decreased substantially, it is a lot less crowded than it used to, and the service must definitely be part of the reason.
I do suggest to ask to be seated upstairs if you are looking to be a little cozy.
For the quality of food, a little too expensive.
